_comment: gen318 (forge-master-plan W0b M-1a, §10.12): the GRANULAR contract catalog. tests/benchmarks.json catalogs whole AREAS ('make test' is one row); this catalogs individual executable CONTRACTS, addressable by id: `make check TEST=routing.agent-search.en`. A contract names its owner, its dependencies, its boot profile, its lane (tier), its isolation, its mechanical oracle and its hard timeout — so a red result names the level that owns it instead of only printing a wrong sentence.
_migration_comment: M-1a requires ONE real contract, not a big-bang migration: every area migrates when a generation TOUCHES it. The rows below are the C1 campaign corpus (§16) — the agent-search routing contracts that gen313-315 fixed, plus the arithmetic collision negative that used to steal them. Unmigrated cases still run inside the 'core' area via tests/run.sh and are NOT silently claimed here.

hermetic-en._comment: the tests/run.sh boot: no kb/ files, no session, no world facts, language pinned to English so a case is deterministic regardless of the developer's OS locale.

script-owned._comment: for oracle_kind 'script-row': the harness sets its own boot (that IS part of the contract it asserts), so the runner injects no env of its own.

contracts.note: gen119 BFS planner. gen313-315 red: compound arithmetic preempted the router and stole this turn.
the same search competence through Italian framing words — one code path, not a duplicated handler.
collision NEGATIVE for the agent-search contracts: pure arithmetic must still be claimed by math. A fix that hands search its turn by weakening arithmetic turns this row red.

gen322 (TODO.md P0): parrot0 FABRICATED a code defect — every correct one-line C function was reported 'Missing semicolon'. Found by talking to it, not by a benchmark: 25/25 code-bench gates were green because no case ever fed the checkers CORRECT code. The syntax claim is now vetoed by cc -fsyntax-only, the oracle that was already in the tree. A misclaim is worse than a wall.

gen327 (TODO.md P4, forge §9.3 B4): the bounded repair loop. Before it, a candidate the oracle REFUTED was abandoned with no diagnosis and no second attempt — a one-shot generator with a test, not an agent. Now: observe (real compile+run) -> STRUCTURED verdict (not_ordered vs not_permutation) -> propose from repair_rule/2 FACTS -> select by blast radius -> act -> re-verify -> stop within N, trace kept. An unrepairable defect is DECLINED naming the real diagnosis: a patch nobody ran is a fabrication.

gen324: TODO.md P2 claimed the evaluator 'cannot execute a LOOP'. The code refuted the plan — gen178 added a bounded while, gen179 a three-clause for. The wall was `s += i`: the one statement almost every loop BODY is made of, which is why every real loop looked like a loop failure. /= and %= by zero must REFUSE, not fabricate an integer.

gen323 (TODO.md P1): the code faculty was REAL but reachable only through an exact C phrasebook — 'explain this code:' answered, 'explain what this does:' walled on the same snippet. The phrasings are now intent_cue FACTS, so the surface grows at runtime. Widening it is safe only because register evidence was tightened in the same generation: prose ('the sky is blue') used to be classified as Python, because `is` is a Python keyword.
the bilingual ratchet: Italian is a row in the KB, never a duplicated C branch. If it passes because a second handler was written, the cuechains ratchet (which may only descend) says so.

gen319 (§15 row 2): the first probe of llmscore_world addressable ALONE. The suite is 127 fresh boots / ~70 s, so answering one hypothesis used to cost 126 probes nobody asked about. `--id` selects; the oracle (command, env, comparison) is unchanged. All 127 rows are addressable — `tests/llmscore_world.sh --list` — and migrate into this catalog as generations touch them.
